milestoneTitle: Phase M9.19a.4 — Drag lift goes brighter, not darker. Apple HIG gestures-and-drag.md §4 says lift = transform: scale(~1.05) + shadow increase; no mention of darkening. The previous drag visual inherited .dash-tile:active's 'color-mix(var(--bg-0) 88%, #000000)' darken because the finger stays held down during a drag — user correctly flagged the tile going darker instead of reading as lifted. Fix: gate the :active darken behind :not(.is-dragging), and give .is-dragging (plus .is-dragging:hover / :active to trump any sticky state) an explicit pure-white background so the lifted tile reads as the brightest and most opaque version of itself. Shadow kept as the elevation signal (24px -14 blur for the deep cast shadow, 8px -6 for the close shadow). Specular highlight bumped 0.70 -> 0.80 alpha to match the extra top-edge brightness a lifted page would have. border-color stays hairline so the page edge doesn't harden.
